comment: Side-by-side per register on synthetic_ocr_vi + brianhuster handwriting. PaddleOCR + RapidOCR added 2026-04-30 in response to user's 'check PaddleOCR again' ask: confirmed both share the generic latin_PP-OCRv5_mobile_rec recognizer, which strips all VN diacritics on every register.

recommendation.printed_clean_or_noisy: tesseract — 0.00 / 0.70 % CER vs vietocr 1.41 / 3.37 %; PaddleOCR distant 3rd at 11.63 / 13.09 %; RapidOCR unusable (drops diacritics)
recommendation.printed_hard_scan: vietocr (slight edge) — 29.00 % vs tesseract 30.34 %; PaddleOCR collapses at 73.40 %
recommendation.handwriting_register: vietocr — 31.82 % CER, 37.5 pp absolute reduction vs tesseract 69.34 %; PaddleOCR 3rd at 59.43 %; RapidOCR effectively non-functional on handwriting line crops (97.20 %)

paddleocr_paddleocr_v5_note: PP-OCRv5 ships no VN-specific recognizer. lang='vi' loads latin_PP-OCRv5_mobile_rec, a generic Latin model that recognizes the alphabet but strips VN tone marks. This is a structural limit, not a config issue — verified by testing default pipeline, doc-prep skipped, standalone TextRecognition, and server-rec model.
rapidocr_note: RapidOCR is the ONNX-runtime port of PaddleOCR. It uses the same recognizer family and exhibits the same diacritic stripping. Docling's ocr_engine='rapidocr' option is therefore not viable for VN.
